title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning
description: An implementation team manipulates big integers without normalizing signs, confuses modular inverse with division, and mistakes a worked toy example for secure key generation. Learners cumulatively produce bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.
Use Euclid and extended Euclid to determine coprimality and modular inverses and identify non-invertible cases.
Trace square-and-multiply cost and state why secret exponent code must use vetted constant-time implementations.
